_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C14: 143 dimension updates, 7 Session B findings, 6 Session D pointers

set.notes: KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: slave of Christ as defining inner identity.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Capacity: bondage to sin as inner condition — volitional capacity enslaved.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: stewardship, trustworthiness, humility as stable inner dispositions.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner equality before God transcending social status.
UNCLASSIFIED → Volitional/Will: singularity of ultimate loyalty — the will cannot serve two masters.

KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: willing Spirit-driven service as inner orientation.
KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed: relational identity in shared service under same Lord.
KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: inner reorientation from bondage to sin to willing service.

UNCLASSIFIED → Spiritual/God-ward: liberation into divine freedom and glory — inner being freed into proper God-ward orientation.

KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed: voluntary submission before God grounded in conscience, reverence, faith.

UNCLASSIFIED → Affective/Emotional: existence of hope as inner anchor in desolation.

record.finding: The bondage registry shows a consistent inner-being polarity: enslavement to disordered desires/sin/fear on one side, willing surrender to God/Christ on the other. Session B should examine this polarity as the governing analytical structure — bondage as the inner-being term for what masters the person.

Reg 114 contains the counter-term to disobedience: [1020-002] names Christ's obedience as the inner act that reverses Adam's. Session B should examine the obedience/disobedience polarity across Reg 114 and Reg 50 (C13) as a structurally significant inner-being opposition.

The submission registry distinguishes two dimensions: relational submission to human authority ([1147-001], Relational/Social) and submission before God ([1148-001], Spiritual/God-ward). Session B should examine whether this distinction is sustained across the broader submission/surrender/yielding vocabulary in C14.

The will registry shows the widest dimension spread in C14, spanning Volitional/Will, Theological/Divine-Human, Affective/Emotional, Spiritual/God-ward, Character/Disposition, Cognitive/Mind, Identity/Selfhood, Moral/Conscience. Session B must begin with sub-cluster taxonomy: which groups name the volitional act itself, which name surrounding conditions, and which entered via functional/grammatical terms.

The halakh (to go/walk) groups ([1235-001] through [1235-006]) form a coherent inner-being sub-cluster: inner movement as metaphor for inner orientation. Session B should analyse this motif — the direction of inner movement as the register of spiritual orientation (toward God, toward idols, toward death).

The paradidōmi (G3860) cluster in Reg 180 ([6835-001] through [6835-012]) spans 12 distinct inner-being uses: betrayal, Christ's passion, voluntary self-giving, judicial delivering over, apostolic transmission, discipleship under persecution, stewardship, continual self-entrustment, self-abandonment to desire, divine delegation, discipline for restoration, obedience to tradition. Session B must treat these 12 groups as a structured taxonomy of 'giving over/handing on' as an inner-being category.

The dōron/didōmi gift-vocabulary sub-cluster in Reg 180 shows a consistent pattern: giving as the domain where inner character is most directly revealed — generosity, greed, love, indifference, dependence, and gratitude all surface through the giving action. Session B should examine giving as a primary inner-being diagnostic.

record.description: The bondage vocabulary (doulos family) maps directly onto the disobedience vocabulary (Reg 50, C13) as structural complements — disobedience names the inner act of refusal, bondage names the resulting or prior inner state of captivity. Session D should examine the relationship between Reg 17 (bondage), Reg 50 (disobedience), and Reg 173 (will) as a unified inner-volitional domain.

Reg 137 (resolve) has zero verse context groups — no inner-being verse evidence exists for this domain. Session D should assess whether 'resolve' as an inner-being category is implicitly present in the will, obedience, and bondage registries without dedicated vocabulary, and whether a Phase 2 research pass is warranted to identify resolve-relevant verses.

Reg 156 (surrender) contains only one group and it describes the closing of the heart against compassion — the opposite of surrender. The term H5462 sa.gar entered via XREF connection. Session D should assess whether the surrender registry requires reconceptualisation in Phase 2, or whether this single group should be examined for reassignment.

The inheritance sub-cluster in Reg 173 ([3094-001], [3094-002], [3095-001], [3095-002], [3095-003]) is analytically adjacent to the justice/righteousness vocabulary in C13 (Reg 98). Session D should examine whether 'inheritance' as an inner-being category belongs primarily in the volitional cluster (C14) or the moral-accountability cluster (C13).

Reg 180 (yielding) has the broadest semantic range in C14: giving vocabulary (dōron family), sowing vocabulary (za.ra/ze.ra), bearing vocabulary (ferō), and water imagery (hudōr). Session D should assess whether yielding as an inner-being category is sufficiently coherent to sustain a single registry, or whether it spans multiple distinct domains (self-surrender, productive fruitfulness, generous giving, patient endurance).

The water groups ([6836-001] through [6836-008]) cluster around inner transformation, inner thirst/satisfaction, and conscience cleansing. Session D should examine whether the water imagery vocabulary belongs in the yielding registry or has stronger affinity with inner transformation vocabulary in Reg 26 (conscience, C13) and the guilt/repentance registries.
